positiveClaims: The existing release claim publication gate is product-bound as bounded internal Review Bridge 12H publication gate admission.
Accepted 12H requires raw 12G boundary input and re-evaluates the 12G user-facing boundary gate internally before accepting publication gate admission.
Accepted 12H requires the internally evaluated 12G user-facing boundary result to have accepted boundary type, status, code, and reason.
Accepted 12H requires the internally evaluated 12G boundary binding fields for mode, claimId, dossierId, matrixId, and releaseClass.
Accepted 12H requires the internally evaluated 12G boundary summary fields for claimSurface, packetId, and attestationId.
Accepted 12H requires requestedMode to match the accepted 12G boundary mode.
Accepted 12H requires requestedClaimSurface to match the accepted 12G boundary claimSurface.
Stale or fabricated boundaryResult input is not accepted as provenance; when boundaryResult is supplied, it must match the internally re-evaluated 12G result.
PR_MODE USER_FACING publication gate requests are blocked through the 12G boundary gate chain.
RELEASE_MODE USER_FACING publication gate admission is accepted only when the accepted 12G boundary releaseClass is USER_FACING_CLAIM_READY.
Accepted 12H emits a deterministic non-mutating publication gate result with fixed top-level, binding, and summary keys.
The 12H publication runtime now strips inherited prototype fields before publication input and boundaryResult acceptance checks.

This contour is release claim publication gate truth only; it is not product publication, release readiness, release execution, command admission, kernel fence, import, export, apply, storage truth, or project truth.
nonClaims: No release execution completion is claimed.
No release publication completion is claimed.
No release-wide publication authority is claimed.
No Google Docs support is claimed.
No DOCX support expansion is claimed.
No review import completion is claimed.
No manuscript write is performed by release claim publication gate binding.
No project truth write is performed by release claim publication gate binding.
No receipt or recovery evidence is created by release claim publication gate binding.
No DOCX import safe-create behavior is changed.
No DOCX export behavior is changed.
No import/export MVP closeout is widened.
No PDF, EPUB, HTML, Mindmap, cloud sync, cross-platform readiness, packaged readiness, or Y9 admission is claimed.
layerDecisions: Publication gate binding is gate admission only.
Publication gate binding does not publish anything.
Publication gate binding does not become project truth by itself.
Publication gate binding does not write manuscript truth or project truth.
Publication gate binding does not execute release commands.
Publication gate binding does not admit command execution.
Publication gate binding does not admit kernel fence.
USER_FACING_CLAIM_READY remains an internal packet, boundary, and publication-gate class only, not a release readiness state.
USER_FACING is a boundary and publication gate surface in this contour, not a user-facing UI state.
This contour is not a release execution contour.
This contour is not a product publication contour.
This contour is not content import and does not create scenes.
This contour is not Review import automation and does not activate a Review session.
This contour is not an apply plan and does not authorize mutation.
This contour is not safe write and does not create receipt or recovery evidence.
This contour is not export and does not alter DOCX output.
Review Bridge may reference accepted 12H output as bounded internal chain evidence, but accepted 12H output does not become project truth.

machineChecks.command: node --test release claim publication gate kernel contracts
node --test release claim publication gate binding contracts
node --test release claim publication gate kernel plus binding contracts
node --test release claim publication gate binding plus adjacent 12B through 12H contracts
node --test release claim downstream 12I through 12K contracts
node --test release claim full contract pack

agentAudits.finding: Found that a fabricated accepted-looking boundaryResult could previously drive 12H accepted without real 12G provenance. Resolved by requiring raw boundaryInput, internally re-evaluating 12G, adding boundaryResult mismatch blocking, and adding synthetic/stale/prototype negative contracts.

Confirmed 12H kernel existed without dedicated binding status, governance approval, or docs sync. Required status, nonclaims, layer decisions, docs updates, and release-readiness overclaim guards were added for publication gate admission only.

Confirmed fabricated plain and stale boundaryResult inputs no longer bypass raw 12G re-evaluation, then found supplied non-plain boundaryResult values were ignored despite the written consistency claim. Resolved by blocking any supplied non-plain boundaryResult as BOUNDARY_RESULT_MISMATCH and adding a targeted contract.

nextStep: complete delivery chain, then select the next owner-approved boundary explicitly; command admission, kernel fence, real publication, and post-MVP import/export expansion do not open by implication
